text.title: Gran Canaria Travel Guide 2025 🇮🇨 Top Attractions + Secret Spots
text.u: imagine a land where lush green forest
gives way to vast deserts and Volcanic
Peaks where pastel hu Villages dot the
landscape and you constantly surrounded
by the Sea no this isn't a dream but the
Lush and verdant island of gr
Canaria welcome to Gran Canaria this is
a second most visited of the Canary
Islands after tenar and over the next
few days we're going to show you the
best things to do on the island one of
archipelago of islands which is situated
off the west coast of North Africa in
the Atlantic Ocean Gran Canaria is a
dream destination all year round thanks
to its temporate climate and wealth of
activities so without further Ado let's
get into the best of the
[Music]
island welcome to arukas we're outside
of the Church of St John the Baptist
which dates back to the early 1900s and
is neogothic in style there's plent
plenty of things to see in the town
including a rum Distillery lots of
little streets boutiques and even a
Botanical Garden as soon as you park up
in aras's main car park you'll already
be greeted by its main Monument The
Towering main Church from there it's an
easy walk through pedestrianized streets
to reach other attractions in town on
Route you can even pick up a pamphlet
from the tourist
[Music]
office
there are a surprising number of museums
in town and one is dedicated entirely to
Stone quarrying we grabbed a sandwich
from one of the local cafes which were
surprisingly tasty before heading into
the central Green Park the Municipal
Park is conveniently located close to
the aruka sign and covers around 2.5
acres full of plants and paved walkways
arukas is sometimes referred to as the
town of flowers and in this Oasis you'll
soon see why this is the main Museum in
town and is where you can find a lot of
gorgeous artworks including sculptures
in the basement is also where you can
learn more about the history of this
town and the surrounding
[Music]
region All Around Town you'll find some
pretty beautiful architectural quirks
and I love spying the cathedral from
different angles the historic buildings
and even a few fountains we're now on
our way to the local R Distillery the
final attraction in arukas I want to
draw your attention to is the arukas rum
Distillery founded in 18 1983 as a
sugarcane Refinery just a year later the
rum Distillery opened its doors today
you can visit and take a tour for a
small fee or tentatively visit the shop
and pick up a souvenir from your time in
the
[Music]
town around a 5minute driveway you'll
find another Botanical treasure we've
just arrived at a small Botanical Garden
on the fringes of arukas by the name of
Mara Gardens they date all the way back
to 1880 and so let's go and explore them
the car park is free and from there
you'll walk through a floral tunnel to
be transported into a garden Paradise
that will take around an hour or so to
[Music]
explore once you've purchased your entry
ticket you're free to explore at your
leisure we visited the Garden in January
and thanks to the spring-like climate of
grand Canaria there were things in bloom
even then however I would probably
suggest that the best time to actually
visit the gardens is between April and
June and then September through to
October
[Music]
if you love bird eye perspectives then
before you leave the arukas region
you'll want to head up to the recently
renovated Mirador de la monana de arukas
provided it's a good day you'll be
rewarded with stunning views of the Town
its Cathedral and the surrounding area
there's a shaded picnic spot ample
parking and even a few signs explaining
the history of arukas
it was then a 20-minute drive to reach
our next destination on Route we soaked
up the rich and varied landscape that
makes this island so
special welcome to cobio De valeron
which was a communal Granary used here
in the Canary Islands until the 15th
century today it's an archaeological
site you can visit for just €3 there's a
free car park at the base of the steps
up to reach the archaeological site
senobio actually means Monastery but
this is a misnomer from a false legend
that young highclass women slept there
until they were ready for marriage high
up on the Mont Del Galo this settlement
and storage area was built by
prehispanic canarians 800 years ago
before the arrival of the Spanish they
made use of the rich volcanic rock
carving out storage base with
Flintstones the result is over 350
cavities laid out over several
levels the entire VIs will probably only
take you about 20 minutes if you read
all of the panels and go inside the
different areas but it's honestly so
fascinating and amazing to see the caves
carved out of the rock face that it's
definitely worth a
[Music]
detour it was then time to drive to our
final Stu of the day in order to see a
little more history and taste some of
the local
[Music]
food galar is a quintessentially
Canarian town with pastel hued houses
gorgeous architecture and handful of
museums you can easily walk around for
an hour or so enjoying the views from
the town's strategic vantage point and
snapping photos however the main
attraction here happens to be one of the
most important archaeological sites in
Spain this is where you'll find a museum
dedicated to the qua pintada I.E The
Painted Cave local Legend tells that a
farmer found the cave quite by accident
while tending to his land in the 1860s
The Painted Cave was officially marked
on the map when it was discovered by an
investigator named Jose R in 1873
unfortunately you can't take photos or
videos of the cave for copyright reasons
and so you have to search pictures
online it's basically a gorgeous set of
geometric patterns that was painted by
the canary ey between the 11th and 13th
centuries it's so unique that it is
regarded to be the Cy Chapel of the
Canary Islands surrounding the cave is
the archaeological site of a former
prehispanic Canarian settlement
including some reconstructed
[Music]
homes
we then headed to a lively nearby tapest
bar to sample some local food washed
down with an ice cold beer the bartender
was really friendly it was warm enough
to sit outside with a jacket and I'll
probably be dreaming about the olives
for years to
[Music]
come we ended the day with a beautiful
sunset over the town of
agete
we've just arrived in fargas which is a
village in a mountains around half an
hour away from Las Palmer the village is
also known as the village of water and
is also known for the Paso de gr caneria
which is a walkway running through the
entirety of the village and is one of
the most beautiful places to walk in the
Canary
Islands
the Villager strategic Vantage Point
High in the mountains also means that
there are a number of beautiful
viewpoints which are known locally as
miradors one of the more historic
buildings in town is the church which is
situated on Plaza Des Sam rocki free to
visit it was built in
[Music]
we then had a long and lazy lunch where
we indulged in local foods such as oine
drizzled in Honey deep fried cheese and
cold beers afterwards it was time to
explore the rest of the town before
heading to our next stop we've just
arrived outside of the Molino de free
grass which is a Watermill dating all
the way back to the 16th century it was
formerly linked to the sugar refinery in
the village and has now been transformed
into a small Museum that you can go
inside for free and check out
[Music]
the final attraction in town west
checking out is a set of mosaics
depicting scenes of the Canary Islands
while you're surrounded by the sound of
a gently flowing
[Music]
river
[Music]
welcome to Tero which is considered to
be one of the most beautiful villages in
Grand Canaria it's full of cobbled lanes
and traditional Canarian architecture
there's lots of little cafes and
boutiques here to explore it's in a
valley surrounded by high peaks and so
even if the area above is clouded down
here it's quite Pleasant and clear I
would personally set aside around 2
hours to explore the town as there's
definitely a lot of beautiful
architecture some really lovely cafes
where you can sit and watch the world go
by and a few main attractions this
Basilica is dedicated to the Virgin Mary
of the Pines I was built between 1760
and 1767 as you can see is neog gothical
and barck in style but it's closed
unfortunately until 400 p.m. as it
closes during the middle of the day and
I think I saw a chicken over
there I think we found the source of the
chickens there SC the man one no don't
scare
[Music]
it inside the church itself at the back
you'll find this small Museum of sacred
art unfortunately it's only open from
Tuesday till Saturday 10:30 to 2:30 p.m.
and so we've missed the opening directly
behind the Basilica building you'll find
the Episcopal Palace which is a former
Bishop's house it has now been
transformed into a cult Cal Center that
hosts art exhibitions and the
[Music]
like as much as I didn't want to we then
had to leave to visit the next place in
this
guide we've just arrived at the
Botanical Gardens of Gran Canaria
they're completely free to visit and
with a life works of 20th century
Swedish Spanish botanist Eric Ragnar
senson they're really beautiful and full
of over 500 succulent and cacti
[Music]
species the Canary Island is pretty
diverse in its climates and Grand canera
has a lot of different microclimates
this area was decided for the Botanical
Gardens and it was reason that it's
probably one of the areas on crank area
that has the best chance of supporting
the greatest number of different plants
from different climbers across the
islands set aside around half an hour to
visit the gardens they make this an hour
if you're visiting in spring or summer
when everything is in
bloom our final stop of the day was also
where we happened to base ourselves for
the first half of our stay in Gran
Canaria Les palas is one of the capitals
of the Canary Islands together with
Santa Cruz to tene there are around
378,000 th residents making this the
ninth largest city in Spain the best
area to explore is hands down the fua
district a historic area filled with
coupled lanes and traditional
architecture here you'll find plenty of
quintessential restaurants museums and a
[Music]
market and after all that exploration
was done we treated ourselves with an
alcohol-free beer at the maard Del
Puerto the next day the weather was
bright and sunny honestly the weather
changed so frequently here that our
weather prediction was often wrong for
the day so we decided it was time to
enjoy the viewpoints of the island if
you're not really into beaches then you
don't really need to worry as there are
so many beautiful landscapes that you
can explore our first stop was at the
Viewpoint of Zamora you can see an old
washing station here as well as admire
the view and even grab a coffee at the
cafe just down the road you can soak up
the visors from meor D Miguel unamuno
where you can see all the way out to the
ocean there's ample parking here so you
could even enjoy a picnic in
[Music]
situ this is one of the best viewpoints
of the north of the island and as you
can see we're so high up that there's
extensive cloud coverage covering Las
palas nevertheless if you head here on a
good day put the name on the screen then
you will expect to find some beautiful
views to be honest the mountains of
central Gran Canaria are just
mesmerizing and you won't be able to
help but stop to take photos at many
viewpoints along the
[Music]
way one of the most Charming of the
mountain villages in the central part of
Gran Canaria is a small settlement by
the name of tinara this is cran Canary's
highest Village and sits on a giant
natural balcony surrounded by rocky
mountain peak weeks we decided to stop
for lunch here at Araya which even had
vegan and vegetarian dishes on the menu
like most of Gran caneria the main
Church in town is free to visit and
boast a belfrey made of redstone from
tamadaba
[Music]
Wander away from the village enter and
there are a few other Delights to
discover the lookout dedicated to D
Miguel de unamuno provides one of the
most gorgeous views in town and is
perfect for snapping a souvenir photo
from there's even a life-size statue of
the Spanish writer and
philosopher
there's also a small Museum depicting
what life was like in rural grank
Canaria many years ago but truthfully
the best things to do is simply tooll
around and allow the views to reveal
themselves to
[Music]
you if you head to the edge of the
village then you'll find a small Chapel
carved out of the rock face dedicated to
the virgin of the cave
we're currently 1,000 m above sea level
in a little village called AA which is
listed as one of the most beautiful
Villages of Spain we're currently in the
very heart of grand Canaria overlooking
the rest of the island and this little
village is known for its beautiful
traditional Canarian architecture as
well as beast and
[Music]
bars
Taha has a population of just under
2,000 residents and is located on the
Eastern edge of a volcanic crater that
bears the same
[Music]
name the historic Center is really
pretty and is full of narrow cold lanes
that can only be explored on foot you'll
only need around half an hour to see
everything although you w't set aside an
hour if you want to have a drink at one
of the cafes near the ch Church the
entirety of the town is surrounded by
the sacred mountains which is a world
heritage site and just adds the beauty
of the place
[Music]
hello everywhere we've been has been so
full of cats I keep getting distracted
by them as we're filming I think we
should rename this video cats and Gran
[Music]
Canaria it was then time to bid farewell
to haa to go up the very scary mountain
roads to go to the highest peak on the
[Music]
island this is Pico deanes and at
1,949 m above sea level is the highest
point on Gren
[Music]
area okay actually this is the highest
point on the island and the whole area
gets its name because of the old Wells
which used to be used to store melted
snow it's probably actually that point
but you can't go
there we then drove back down the
mountain through the scary mountain
roads to our second hotel in the resort
town of pler deling which is a great
base from which to explore the south of
the island after checking in we feasted
on Spanish tapas dishes made vegetarian
at a delightful if a bit slow restaurant
by the name of el Lagoon after an early
night the next day was spent doing a
different activity which I haven't
mentioned yet and that is hiking
[Music]
welcome to Branco de guya de which is a
ravine with steep slopes stretching up
to 300 M in height the peculiarity of
the houses in this Valley is that
they're built straight into the Cliff
face there's also a unique Cafe here
which is built inside a cave so let's go
and check it
[Music]
out
[Music]
it's really amazing to see all of the
rock homes but there's one mistake that
a tourist has just done that I highly
advise against and that is peering
straight into people's homes trying
their doors and going in their front
garden so just kind of Admire from a
distance if you come
[Music]
here unfortunately there was a bit of
the weight at the restaurant there was a
few couples waiting no one seemed to be
being served and we have a lot of things
to do today so we're going to try the
other restaurant just up the hill and if
that's also really busy then I think
we're going to leave and just get a
sandwich so that we can carry on the
[Music]
itinerary we've just arrived at
Restaurante El Centro which setes up a
number of Spanish Papa dishes and there
are a few vegetarian options on the menu
we're actually inside a cave and so it's
pretty cool in here all year
round
we've just driven around 10 minutes
parked up the car and we're at the next
Ravine along which is called Banco de
las vas many people online said it's
reminiscent of the Landscapes of Utah so
let's go and check it
[Music]
out we found the way down it was a bit
tricky but an spotted
it you'll need around 15 minutes to hike
down into the Ravine and make sure to
wear comfortable shoes as the trail it's
pretty Rocky and uneven in places
there's a lot of Corners around here
that look like they've been used as
toilets so definitely watch your step I
actually didn't think we were in the
right place because we couldn't see any
of the canyon like rock forms that I'd
seen online however what you need to do
is carry on along the valley and go
under the bridge from there you'll start
to enjoy the rock forms there are a few
things I should say about this place
including that it's very very touristy
and so you won't be the only people
there be a good tourist I think that
goes without saying take your trash with
you don't def face the rock faces and be
kind and courteous to other
[Music]
[Music]
visitors welcome to the junas deas
Palomas which is a 404 hectare area of
sand Junes they're actually preserved
and so a lot of them can't be walked on
but there are guided Trails you can
follow to enjoy all of the
[Music]
Junes I personally think that the best
time to visit these sand Tunes is at
dusk when you can enjoy all of the
beautiful candy colors dancing across
the sky and across the
[Music]
Junes there are a few things you should
know about getting around gr Canaria and
one of the most important is that the
best way to get around is by renting
your own car I would parly recommend
renting the smallest vehicle possible
which is usually a f 500 as there are
lots of narrow corners and some of the
parking situations can be a little
tricky so it's good to have a small car
that can fit in any space this isn't
sponsored or anything but I definitely
recommend using C car we use it each
time we come to the Canary Islands as
it's voted one of the best car rentals
in Europe and is one of the best car
rental experiences we've ever had unless
it stated on a panel outside or inside
the car park most car parks in the
Canary Islands are actually free to stay
in especially in Grand Canaria however
you should be wory of fake parking
attendants who will kind of direct you
to a space and then get you to pay money
to them my advice would be to ignore
these people park your car up if it's a
free car park and just walk away thank
you so much for watching and don't
forget to like And subscribe for More
Travel movies see you next time
